nodes.text: The Park
EXT. THE PARK — DAY
Faust is jogging with his friend, Jacob.
What have you been reading, my friend?
Oh, let's see — Ratifying Nations of the Comprehensive Nuclear Test Ban Treaty.
I just started reading — funnily enough — the book whose title character I was named after.
So how is it as a translation? Hear us yanks can't quite get it right.
Not too shabby. Taking my time with it. Have this funny feeling life is getting weirder.
Lay off the recreational, man — you're wigging me out a bit. Here.
No big deal. Well — it is a big deal. Found some fun stuff out about my grandparents.
People are always finding out cool stuff about grandparents. Grandparents are awesome for all the shit they lived through.
Well said. But my relations, while awesome, ran to the kooky occult side of things. Dabbled in sex magick and the like.
Go on.
I'm not sure if my grand-dad was Jack Parsons or L. Ron Hubbard.
I know of Monsieur Xenu — but not of this Parsons dude.

opts.text: "Long story short, let me sum it up."
"Buckle up. This one earns its bourbon."

nodes.text: A Ken Burns Montage
Lafayette — as I'll call L. Ron — always wanted to be a sailor boy. Dreamed big dreams. And boy, how he schemed.
Loved his genre books, his science fictions, cowboys, jungles, outer space creatures.
Lived a wonderful life — if only in his own head. Submarine commander and deep-cover (yet decorated) secret agent.
Medical discharge. Drifting about Los Angeles in with New Age, better-living, better-looking people — drawn to his larger-than-life escapades.
They were not all entirely stupid. Jack Parsons was perhaps brilliant — a non-academic rocket scientist, founding father, wicked eccentric.
His tastes ran to the occult. His lofty Pasadena mansion — home to many like-minded explorers, also traversing the edge of existence.
Lafayette and Parsons were best friends, worst enemies — each seeking the same thing from their own extreme, gazing into the abyss of their reflection.
They shared a woman. Her name was Betty. She was my grandmother. And here I am, five decades later.
Cut back to the jogging.
I'll have to read up on that.
Me too. There's more cool shit to it.
Funky family skeletons do that chicken dance.
Tell me about it.
Jacob's Apartment
INT. JACOB'S APARTMENT — DAY. Jacob and Faust playing video games.
So what's been new with you?
Working on cool video games. Smorking. Getting laid regular. Playing some bass. Top Gear torrented for later this evening.
